evals.prompt: /cyoda:app I want to build a task management app
evals.expected_output: Gives brief Cyoda orientation, directs to /cyoda:design, then guides step-by-step through setup → build → test → migrate journey

assertions.text: Gives brief Cyoda orientation for newcomer
Directs user to /cyoda:design for domain brainstorm
Does NOT try to design AND build in the same skill invocation

evals.prompt: /cyoda:app — I already know Cyoda, just want to start building
evals.expected_output: Notes experienced users can skip to individual skills, points directly to /cyoda:build, does not force orientation or design

assertions.text: Notes experienced users can skip directly to individual skills
Does NOT give orientation when user says they already know Cyoda
Does NOT force user through design step when they want to skip

evals.prompt: /cyoda:app — I want to use Cyoda Cloud, not install anything locally
evals.expected_output: Accepts cloud-first approach, guides to /cyoda:setup (cloud mode) then /cyoda:login, does not insist on local installation

assertions.text: Does NOT insist on local cyoda-go installation
